title: limonium acutifolium subsp. tharrosianum
intro: Limonium acutifolium subsp. tharrosianum, also known as Limonium tharrosianum, is a subspecies of the Limonium acutifolium plant, belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family. It is a succulent, perennial plant native to the island of Crete in Greece. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and acting as an antiseptic. Limonium acutifolium subsp. tharrosianum can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, or used in a topical cream or ointment, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal properties and potential uses.
